Title: Getting Started with NUST LMS API

Nodes.Text: You've generated a comprehensive API developer portal that transforms your OpenAPI specification into a complete developer experience. This includes interactive documentation, auto-generated SDKs in multiple languages, live API testing capabilities, and AI-accessible documentation.
API Reference documentation with language‑specific docs and SDK guides. Developers can explore your API structure before writing any code.
Built-in testing interface supporting all major authentication methods. Developers can make real API calls, see actual responses, and validate their integration approach.
Production-ready client libraries in Java, Python, TypeScript, C#, PHP, Ruby, and Go. Each SDK includes comprehensive documentation, code samples, and follows language-specific best practices.
Step-by-step onboarding flows to help developers implement key API usecases faster.
Full control over colors, fonts, logos, and layout. Add custom documentation pages, modify navigation, and ensure the portal reflects your brand identity.
Automatic generation of llms.txt files makes your API discoverable by AI coding assistants. Built-in API copilot answers developer questions using your documentation.
In the next 15 minutes you can add custom guides, create API recipes, apply your branding, enable AI assistance, and optimize for discoverability.
Explore the Advanced Features Guide and unlock your portal's full potential.

Nodes.Title: How quickly can we launch our Developer Portal?

Nodes.Text: You can generate and publish your Developer Portal in minutes. Simply upload your OpenAPI specification, customize the look and feel to match your brand, and publish instantly - either as a standalone site or embedded into your existing Developer Portal.

Nodes.Title: How does the Portal stay current as our API evolves?

Nodes.Text: APIMatic automatically updates your portal when your API specification changes. You can re-import updated API definitions, and the portal will regenerate with the latest endpoints, parameters, and documentation while preserving your customizations.

Nodes.Title: What level of customization and control do we have?

Nodes.Text: You can completely customize the look, feel and behavior of your Developer Experience Portal, including primary colors, themes, custom sections, navigation links, and branding. You can also choose to host it on your own custom domain or embed it seamlessly into your existing documentation site using a simple HTML script.

Nodes.Title: Is there a way we can integrate this with our existing Developer Portal?

Nodes.Text: Absolutely. APIMatic provides embeddable portal components that can be seamlessly integrated into your existing documentation sites using a simple HTML script. You can also choose to host the portal separately on your own domain while maintaining your existing portal structure.

Nodes.Title: What AI capabilities does this Developer Portal have?

Nodes.Text: APIMatic's Developer Portal includes two key AI features: an API copilot that can answer questions about your API based on information from the API reference documentation and product documentation, and automatic LLMs.txt file generation that makes your documentation AI-accessible to tools like Cursor, GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT, and Claude.

NUST Learning Management System API for developer integrations (Moodle AJAX wrapper). Each path is virtual for clean SDK generation; NustDevKit resolves them to the underlying Moodle AJAX service endpoint transparently.
The API uses the following base URI:
https://lms.nust.edu.pk/portal

This API uses the following authentication schemes.
* [`SessKey (Custom Query Parameter)`]($h/__auth_CustomQueryAuthentication)
This API uses the following query parameters for authentication.

Data: Session key obtained after authenticating with the LMS portal.

Nodes.Text: The request looks like this:
curl https://lms.nust.edu.pk/portal?sesskey={SESSKEY}

GET /?sesskey={SESSKEY} HTTP/1.1

Nodes.Title: Get Core Recent Courses
Nodes.Name: getCoreRecentCourses
Nodes.Description:
Returns the courses the authenticated user has accessed most recently, ordered by last access time.
Moodle method: `core_course_get_recent_courses`

Parameters.Name: body
Parameters.Description: Parameters for the recent courses request.

Nodes.Title: Get Calendar Events by Timesort

Moodle method: `core_calendar_get_action_events_by_timesort`

Parameters.Name: body
Parameters.DataType: Get Calendar Events by Timesort Request
Parameters.Description: Parameters for the time-sorted calendar events request.

Fields.Name: limitnum
Fields.Description: Maximum number of events to return.

Fields.Name: timesortfrom
Fields.Description: Only return events with timesort >= this Unix timestamp. Pass 0 for no lower bound.

Fields.Name: timesortto
Fields.Description: Only return events with timesort <= this Unix timestamp. Optional.

Fields.Name: aftereventid
Fields.Description: Return events whose ID is greater than this value (cursor pagination). Optional.

Nodes.Title: Custom Field
Nodes.Name: CustomField
Nodes.Description:
A custom profile field value attached to a user.

*This model accepts additional fields of type Object.*

Fields.Name: name
Fields.Description: Unique key identifying the preference.

Fields.Name: value
Fields.Description: Preference value as a string. Note that the internal _lastloaded preference is returned as an integer by the LMS.

Nodes.Title: User Preference
Nodes.Name: UserPreference
Nodes.Description:
A single user preference setting.

*This model accepts additional fields of type Object.*

Fields.Name: id
Fields.Description: Unique numeric user identifier.

Fields.Name: username
Fields.Description: Login username for the user.

Fields.Name: fullname
Fields.Description: Full display name of the user.

Fields.Name: email
Fields.Description: Email address of the user.

Fields.Name: department
Fields.Description: Department the user belongs to within the institution.

Fields.Name: institution
Fields.Description: Name of the institution the user is affiliated with.

Fields.Name: idnumber
Fields.Description: Institution-assigned student or staff ID number.

Fields.Name: auth
Fields.Description: Authentication method used by the user (e.g., 'ldap', 'manual').

Fields.Name: confirmed
Fields.Description: Whether the user's account has been confirmed.

Fields.Name: lang
Fields.Description: Preferred interface language code (e.g., 'en').

Fields.Name: theme
Fields.Description: Preferred LMS theme; empty string means use the site default.

Fields.Name: timezone
Fields.Description: Timezone identifier. "99" means use the server default.

Fields.Name: mailformat
Fields.Description: Email format preference (1 = HTML).

Fields.Name: profileimageurlsmall
Fields.Description: URL to the user's small profile image (thumbnail).

Fields.Name: profileimageurl
Fields.Description: URL to the user's full-size profile image.

Fields.Name: customfields
Fields.Description: List of custom profile field values for the user.

Fields.Name: preferences
Fields.Description: List of stored preference key/value pairs for the user.

Fields.Name: id
Fields.Description: Unique notification identifier.

Fields.Name: useridfrom
Fields.Description: User ID of the sender.

Fields.Name: useridto
Fields.Description: User ID of the recipient.

Fields.Name: subject
Fields.Description: Subject line of the notification.

Fields.Name: shortenedsubject
Fields.Description: Shortened version of the notification subject for compact display.

Fields.Name: text
Fields.Description: Short HTML excerpt of the notification.

Fields.Name: fullmessage
Fields.Description: Full plain-text body of the notification.

Fields.Name: fullmessageformat
Fields.Description: Format identifier for fullmessagehtml (2 = Markdown source).

Fields.Name: fullmessagehtml
Fields.Description: Full HTML body of the notification email.

Fields.Name: smallmessage
Fields.Description: Short plain-text preview of the notification.

Fields.Name: contexturl
Fields.Description: URL to the relevant context for this notification (e.g., a forum post).

Fields.Name: contexturlname
Fields.Description: Human-readable label for the context URL link.

Fields.Name: timecreated
Fields.Description: Unix timestamp when the notification was created.

Fields.Name: timecreatedpretty
Fields.Description: Human-readable relative time string (e.g., '19 days ago').

Fields.Name: timeread
Fields.Description: Unix timestamp when the notification was read; null if unread.

Fields.Name: read
Fields.Description: Whether the notification has been read by the recipient.

Fields.Name: deleted
Fields.Description: Whether the notification has been deleted by the recipient.

Fields.Name: iconurl
Fields.Description: URL to the icon image representing the notification source.

Fields.Name: component
Fields.Description: Moodle component that generated the notification (e.g., 'mod_forum').

Fields.Name: eventtype
Fields.Description: Event type within the component that triggered the notification (e.g., 'posts').

Fields.Name: customdata
Fields.Description: JSON-encoded string with additional notification metadata (cmid, instance, discussionid, postid, etc.).
